hypotheses.description: Add trading-hour window to SSS: restrict entries to 08:00â€“20:00 UTC. The SHAP analysis shows hour_cos (0.345) and hour_sin (0.240) are the top-2 features by a wide margin, and the decoded actionable rule (hour_sin<0.75 AND hour_cos<0.63) maps to roughly 09â€“21 UTC â€” the London+NY liquidity window. Gold is a macro asset that trends during institutional hours; during Asian session, XAU/USD mean-reverts and SSS breakout entries get chopped up. Currently SSS has zero hour filtering, meaning it generates signals during low-liquidity overnight hours that drag win rate down. The asian_breakout strategy already uses london_entry_start/end_utc â€” SSS should too.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: The weak-filter rule shows 36.2% win rate vs 30.3% baseline (1.20x lift, n=80) when filtering by hour. Adding a clean hour window should capture most of this lift â€” expect +4-6pp win rate improvement. This is the single highest-impact change available since time features dominate the SHAP ranking.

hypotheses.description: Raise min_confluence_score from 2 to 3. SHAP shows confluence_score at 0.071 importance â€” moderate but non-trivial. More critically, it interacts with the time features: the low-confluence trades that squeak through at score=2 are disproportionately the off-peak, low-quality entries. The current scoring (0â€“8 scale, max: 2+2+1+1+1+1) means score=2 only requires SS alignment OR ISS alignment with nothing else â€” that's a one-leg signal with no confirmation. Score=3 forces at least one additional confluence factor (CBC, 50% tap, target proximity, or no F2 conflict). Since signal_tier has near-zero SHAP (0.011), the tier system isn't adding much, but the raw confluence threshold is a meaningful gate.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: Expect to filter out ~20-30% of the weakest trades (those with only single-leg alignment and no confirming factors). Win rate should improve by +2-3pp at the cost of fewer signals. Net profit factor improvement of ~0.1-0.2. Lower confidence because the SHAP importance for confluence is moderate â€” the hour filter is doing most of the heavy lifting.

hypotheses.description: Add day-of-week exclusion for low-edge days. The hour_cos Ã— dow_sin interaction (0.189) is the strongest pairwise interaction in the entire SHAP matrix â€” stronger than any single feature except hour_cos itself. dow_sin = sin(2Ï€Â·dow/7) peaks mid-week (Wed/Thu) and troughs at week boundaries (Mon/Fri-Sun). Gold exhibits known behavioral patterns: Monday is range-establishing (institutional flow re-pricing from weekend gaps), Friday afternoon sees position-squaring ahead of the weekend. The interaction with hour_cos suggests that the London-NY session edge is strongest Tue-Thu and weakest Mon/Fri. Excluding or reducing exposure on Monday and Friday would align with the SHAP evidence and known gold market microstructure.
hypotheses.expected_improvement: Stacking the day-of-week filter on top of the hour window should capture the interaction effect. The interaction SHAP (0.189) is nearly as large as dow_sin alone (0.244), meaning most of the day-of-week signal comes from its interplay with hour â€” confirming that day+hour together matter more than either alone. Expect an additional +2-3pp win rate improvement on top of the hour filter, at the cost of ~40% fewer trading days (losing Mon+Fri). Net: fewer trades but higher quality.
